Name: Categorical Exclusion: Grazing Lease Renewal Sahara Draw Allotment
nepa_project_description__c: **A. Description of the Proposed Action: Background The Bureau of Land Management (BLM), Buffalo Field Office (BFO) proposes to renew a 10 - year grazing lease for the Sahara Draw allotment (#22110). The Sahara Draw allotment is about 17 miles northeast of Buffalo Wyoming in Sheridan County. The allotment consists of about 10,750 total acres of which 1% is BLM land, 36% state of Wyoming lands, and 63% is deeded land. The lease authorizing grazing on the allotment includes a total of 121 acres of BLM land and 20 animal unit months (AUMs) of forage. Grazing use is authorized for cattle from 3/1-2/28. The Sahara Draw allotment is a "Custodial" allotment, meaning BLM management is impractical due to the combination of size and spacing of the BLM administered surface within the larger private ranch. The BLM parcels are listed below and shown on the attached map: T. 53 N., R. 80 W. Sec. 4: N½SE¼ T. 53 N., R. 81 W. Sec. 13: SW¼SW¼  The current grazing lessee owns or controls the base property associated with the allotment and currently hold the grazing authorization for that allotment. The lease #4907181 was last issued on May 14, 2014 and it expires on December 31, 2019. The lease is being fully processed and issued again due to the completion of the Rangeland Health Assessment in April 2018.  The current lessee has applied for renewal and/or issuance of the grazing lease authorizing grazing on the allotment associated with their lease. Per 43 CFR 4110, the grazing lessee has preference in retaining the grazing privileges attached to each property. If the proposed action is implemented, a new term grazing lease will be offered to the lessee.  A formal Standards for Healthy Rangelands and Guidelines for Livestock Grazing Management for Public Lands Administered by the Bureau of Land Management in the State of Wyoming (S&G) assessment was completed on the Sahara Draw allotment in April 2018. The assessment found that the allotment is meeting all the applicable standards. BLM distributed the final S&G report to all interested persons, and it is available for review at the Buffalo Field Office.  Proposed Action The proposed action is to offer a new 10 - year term grazing lease to Ridgeline Ranch Operations, LLC (#4907181) for the Sahara Draw allotment (#22110). Table 1 shows the current authorized use for the lease.  Table 1. Mandatory terms and conditions of the existing grazing leases affected by the proposed action Authorization | Allotment | Allotment | Public | % Public | Livestock | Livestock | Season of | AUMS | Type of Number | Number | Name | Acres | Land | Number | Kind | Use | Use 49007181 | 22110 | Sahara Draw | 120 | 85 | 2 | Cattle | 03/1-2/28 | 20 | C  **Note: The percent public land and livestock numbers will be changing from the existing lease. BLM identified boundary inaccuracies with the allotment so the percent public land and number Bureau of Land Management DOI-BLM-WY-P070-2018-0126-CX Page 1  of head will change on the grazing leases, however the total AUMs on the allotment will stay the same. Table 2 shows the adjustment to the grazing lease due to the administrative errors. This is administrative correction is similar to an administrative maintenance action not needing any further analysis; see 43 CFR 1610.5-4. Table 2 Administrative Changes to % Public land and Livestock Number from the “Mandatory Terms and Conditions” of the Existing Lease Authorization | Allotment | Allotment | Public | % Public | Livestock | Livestock | Season of | AUMs | Type of Number | Number | Name | Acres | Land | Number | Kind | Use | Use 4907181 | 22110 | Sahara Draw | 121 | 1 | 170 | Cattle | 03/1-2/28 | 20 | C  The "other terms and conditions" for the lease are listed below. These ensure the lease conforms to the goals and objectives of the Buffalo RMP. • This authorization is subject to cancellation, suspension, or modification for any violation of the regulations at 43 CFR Part 4100, or of the terms and conditions of the authorization • The terms and conditions of your lease may be modified if additional information indicates that revision is necessary to conform to 43 CFR 4180 • Lessee agrees to allow authorized officers of the USDI-BLM to enter the leased lands at any time for the purpose of inspection • Please notify BLM if number/kind of livestock or dates of use change  The current grazing lessee is in good standing with the BLM and meets all qualifications for obtaining a grazing lease under 43 CFR 4110.1 and 4110.2. In accordance with 43 CFR 4130.2(a), "Grazing permits or leases shall be issued to qualified applicants to authorize use on the public lands and other lands under the administration of the [BLM] that are designated as available for livestock grazing through land use plans." During the term of the lease or following its expiration, the lease may be modified if information indicates changes in management are needed to ensure the allotment are meeting or progressing towards achieving the S&Gs.**
